title: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents related to a government contract with OpenAI referencing the enterprise-wide deployment of a ChatGPT-powered AI assistant. Documents could include the contract and/or enterprise agreement between Massachusetts and OpenAI.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aarushi Sahejpal

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Aarushi,

You can find the OpenAI contract documents here:

https://www.commbuys.com/bso/external/purchaseorder/poSummary.sda?docId=PO-19-1080-OSD03-SRC01-17283&releaseNbr=31&parentUrl=close

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thank you,

Michelle
617-447-1754

title: Florida DJJ Youth Pay to Stay Policy

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida DJJ Youth Pay to Stay Policy
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of fees and debt for incarcerated youth (i.e., juvenile):

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical and room & board costs or fees for which incarcerated youth (i.e. juvenile) and/or their parents or legal guardians are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding medical copays and room and board fees paid to or owed to this department/office or any facility/agency under its jurisdiction in FY2024. Please include all relevant data regarding medical and room & board costs, including the number of youths (i.e., juveniles) charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed in FY2024.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Jenny Wadhwa

title: AB1388 (Burbank Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: AB1388 (Burbank Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Per AB 1388, I am seeking the department's separation agreements made with officers. The records may be considered non-disclosure agreements or clean-record agreements.  These agreements are now void and must be disclosed under state law. https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=202520260AB1388 

Please provide me with copies of these agreements, on a rolling basis as records are ready for release, that cover 2012 to today's date. 

"This bill would additionally exempt agreements between an employing agency and a peace officer that, among other things, require the agency to destroy, remove, or conceal a record of a misconduct investigation. The bill would declare that its provisions are severable."

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: Public Records Request
communications.communication:
Dear Joey Scott,

Please see the attached documents in response to your Public Records Request for separation agreements that cover 2012 to date.

Thank you,


[cid:image001.png@01DCA646.366D8160]

title: Request to Navy for 2014 budget report of Public Affairs Office

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Navy for 2014 budget report of Public Affairs Office
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The annual budget report for the Navy's Public Affairs Office for the year 2015.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Cumberland County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Cumberland County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Department of Defense support for "Charlie" episode of "Tatort" TV series

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for "Charlie" episode of "Tatort" TV series
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the episode "Charlie" (aired March 2, 2025) of the European TV series "Tatort." Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Tatort," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Tatort: Charlie!" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (Tennessee) (University of Tennessee, Knoxville)

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (Tennessee) (University of Tennessee, Knoxville)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with William S. Hein & Co. relating to the licensing of the “HeinOnline” database;
2) All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; and
3) All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with William S. Hein & Co. for the “HeinOnline” database.

Please do not limit your search to strictly direct agreements or renewals between the institution and William S. Hein & Co., but include any agreements and related documentation through consortia such as Tenn-Share, the Tennessee Academic Library Cooperative (TALC), or Lyrasis.

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of the “HeinOnline” database.

We are an alliance of academic research librarians conducting critical research on library vendor contract terminology related to data privacy to identify and expose significant disparities in language between vendors and institutions. This research is essential for advancing national awareness and advocacy around library patron data privacy rights. We would be grateful for your cooperation in supporting this important initiative.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Michele Gibney.

865-974-5180

communications.subject: UTS-446-2025 - Pre-payment required
communications.communication:
Kent Hoover, documents that may be responsive to your request have been identified. 
  
 The Tennessee Public Records Act allows the University to charge for the labor involved in gathering and producing documents, and to request a prepayment of fifty (50) percent of the estimated labor charge, prior to beginning the process. 
  
 The TPRA stipulates that the first hour of highest-compensated labor be provided at no charge. Therefore, the estimated labor cost for this request is 8.0 hours of labor at $22.57 per hour, for a total of $180.56. 
  
 In order to proceed, you will need to pay $90.28, which is 50% of the estimated total. 
  
 Payment can be made with debit/credit card here. 
  
 Once the University receives payment, the process of gathering the records will begin. 
  
 When the work is complete, the actual labor that was expended in the request will be calculated, and you will be notified of any remaining labor cost. 
  
 Please advise us how you wish to proceed. 
  
 Sincerely, 
  
 Charles Primm
Public Records Manager
UT System Administration
865-974-5180

communications.subject: RE: Tennessee Public Records Act Request #UTS-446-2025
communications.communication:
Hello Charles,

I attempted to send the $90.28 though MuckRock but am unsure if the payment was successful. Can you confirm whether you have received any notification that a payment is being processed?

Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Tennessee Public Records Act Request #UTS-446-2025
communications.communication:
Dear Charles,

Just FYI that an admin at Muckrock responded to my request for assistance and let me know that the payment is being processed. They said, "It looks like your payment did go through successfully. Our team is confirming the correct check address before sending out payment, which should take maybe another day at most."

We will look forward to your next communication regarding the document request and would appreciate a confirmation of payment once you receive it.

Thank you!

title: Moonlight Maze (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Moonlight Maze (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request the first thousand pages of records that your agency has on Moonlight Maze. Please keep in mind the amount of information on Moonlight Maze that has been publicly acknowledged by the US government, including that the Russian hacking group Turla is behind the Moonlight Maze cyberattack.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mitch Poran

To Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby
request the following records: I request the first thousand pages of records
that your agency has on Moonlight Maze. Please keep in mind the amount of
information on Moonlight Maze that has been publicly acknowledged by the US
government, including that the Russian hacking group Turla is behind the
Moonlight Maze cyberattack. The requested documents will be made available to
the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial
purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would
inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would
prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or
CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this
matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20
business days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, Mitch Poran Upload
documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2211829/

**

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harnett County Sheriff)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Harnett County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Ronald A. Moore report
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

-I am requesting the full Ronald Moore interview conducted 4/06/1989 with regards to his movements December 5 through 8, 1978.  The previous response to this request was heavily redacted. I am requesting the report to not be redacted. I am also requesting the dates of any interviews conducted with Ronald Moore prior to 4/06/1989.
-I am requesting all statements made by David Neal Cohn, John Kelly, Michael Bruce and Steve Frechette  pertaining to the night of December 5th into the morning of December 6th 1978 in which the individuals made statements to their roles in bringing Theresa Corley to the Presidential Arms in Franklin MA. 
-I am requesting the documentation regarding Margaret Blaine bringing forth a letter from Donald Moore Sr. on or around the time of  his death

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

GERALDINE HOUDE

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Ronald A. Moore report
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Houde,

This letter responds to your December 29, 2025 request submitted pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, M.G.L. c. 66, § 10.
After a diligent review, the Bellingham Police Department responds as follows:

________________________________
Ronald Moore Interview (April 6, 1989) and Prior Interviews
With respect to the interview of Ronald Moore conducted on April 6, 1989, the Department notes that this report was generated by the Massachusetts State Police. Although a copy exists within this Department’s historical case file for reference purposes, the Bellingham Police Department is not the custodian of that record for purposes of the Public Records Law.
The Department possesses no records of any interviews of Ronald Moore conducted prior to April 6, 1989.
Additionally, the interview report relates to an open and unsolved homicide investigation. Disclosure of unredacted investigative materials would prejudice the possibility of effective law enforcement and is therefore exempt from disclosure pursuant to M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(f). The Public Records Law does not require an agency to release records in an unredacted form where lawful exemptions apply.
________________________________
Statements of David Neal Cohn, John Kelly, Michael Bruce, and Steve Frechette
The requested statements were generated by the Massachusetts State Police as part of a multi-agency investigation involving both an open and unsolved homicide and allegations of sexual assault occurring in Franklin, Massachusetts. Although copies may exist within this Department’s historical files for reference purposes, the records were not created by, nor are they maintained by, the Bellingham Police Department as its own public records. Accordingly, this Department is not the custodian of those records for purposes of the Public Records Law.
Further, the requested materials constitute witness statements and investigative records relating to an open homicide investigation and allegations of sexual assault. Disclosure would prejudice the possibility of effective law enforcement and is therefore exempt pursuant to M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(f).
Additionally, the statements contain highly sensitive personal information concerning alleged sexual assault, witnesses, and third parties. Disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy and is independently exempt from disclosure pursuant to M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(c).
________________________________
Documentation Regarding a Letter from Donald Moore Sr.
We do not have a copy of the letter that Donald SR. wrote. We only have a letter/report/memo from State Police Trooper Brian Brooks documenting his conversation with Margaret Blaine about the letter.
This document forms part of an active and unsolved homicide investigative file and was not authored by the Bellingham Police Department. Disclosure would reveal non-public investigative information and context and would prejudice the possibility of effective law enforcement. Accordingly, the document is exempt from disclosure pursuant to M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(f).
________________________________
Format and Accessibility
All responsive materials in the Department’s possession exist solely in paper form. The Public Records Law does not require the Department to convert historical paper records into electronic, OCR, or screen-reader-accessible formats, nor does it require the creation of new records in response to a request.
________________________________
Prior and Future Access
The Department notes that, in recognition of your status as a family member of the victim, you have previously been permitted to review the investigative case file in person. This accommodation exceeds the requirements of the Public Records Law.
If you wish to review the case file again, you may contact Detective Sergeant Marston to request an appointment, subject to availability and appropriate supervision.
________________________________
For records generated and maintained by the Massachusetts State Police,  you may wish to direct your request directly to that agency, or through your Victim Witness Advocate.
If you believe this response is in error, you may appeal to the Supervisor of Public Records pursuant to 950 CMR 32.08.


Sincerely,

Marianne Mahar
Administrative Assistant
Bellingham Police Department
Ph# 508-657-2872
Fax# 508-966-4669

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Ronald A. Moore report
communications.communication:
Thank you for your response to my FOIA request. I would like to appeal your decision based on the age of this case, 47 years and that a FOIA request in 2022 resulted in then Bellingham MA police Chief Gerard Daigle providing a redacted interview after the request and appeal were reviewed by the Supervisor of Records.
M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26(f does not apply in this case. There has been a significant passage of time. Ronald Moore and Paul Thibodeau are both deceased. The information related to the individuals in the interview is widely known. There is no unwarranted invasion of personal privacy given the context of the unsolved capitol crime and the age of the case. There could potentially be a public benefit in releasing the information to potentially solving the crime and revealing the truth. Release of the statement may prompt others with information to come forward. 
There were investigatory failures in the initial stages of the investigation demonstrated by the fact the Ronald Moore presented himself to the Bellingham Police Department on December 8th, 1978 and inquired if it was "Theresa Corley's body on the side of the highway." According to the dispatcher at the time there was no reason Ronald Moore would have had that information since police officers had not gotten to the scene and the information on the discovery of the body was not widely broadcasted. It took 11 years for Ronald Moore to be interviewed by state investigators.  Bellingham PD has attested to in this FOIA request, that there are no other interviews of Ronald Moore. However, interviews were conducted by former Lt. Kevin Ranieri. Lt Ranieri has stated that "Ronnie would get picked up often" and at that point in time he wanted to "talk about Theresa Corley."It would seem if you have no documentation of those encounters then there was a lapse in investigatory effort and knowledge of such would be in the best interests of the public. 
I formally rescind the FOIA request of the individuals at the Presidential Arms, John Kelly, Steve Frechette, Michael Bruce and David Cohn. 
I formally rescind the FOIA request for the letter from Donald Moore Sr. given to Margaret Blaine.

communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Please be aware, this office has received an appeal relating to your entity's response to a request for public records. Attached are further details concerning this appeal. If you have any questions or wish to provide further information relating to this matter, please contact the Public Records Division at pre@sec.state.ma.us<mailto:pre@sec.state.ma.us> or 617-727-2832. Given that the Supervisor of Records must issue a determination within 10 business days of receipt of the appeal petition, please provide any additional information to this office and the requestor as soon as possible.

Thank you,
Jeff Gottfredsen

Senior Attorney | Public Records Division
Office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth
One Ashburton Place, Room 1719 | Boston, MA 02108
617-727-2832 | Jeffrey.Gottfredsen@sec.state.ma.us

communications.subject: FW: SPR26/0251 Appeal Acknowledgement- Additional Information from Bellingham PD
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

This is the response that went to the SPR. Please see below and the attached.

Thank you,

Marianne Mahar
Administrative Assistant
Bellingham Police Department
Ph# 508-657-2872
Fax# 508-966-4669





Good Afternoon,

I am writing briefly to provide additional context in support of the Department’s attached response.
Since I assumed command of the Bellingham Police Department in 2023, this matter has remained an active and ongoing investigation. During this period, the Department has continued investigative activity, including the collection of DNA from persons of interest or individuals potentially connected to persons of interest, receipt and follow-up on both telephone and in-person tips, preparation of investigative summaries, and interviews of potential witnesses.
In addition, the requestor has previously been afforded unfettered, supervised access to review the materials maintained by the Bellingham Police Department in connection with this case. That accommodation was provided in good faith and exceeded the Department’s obligations under the Public Records Law.
The Department remains mindful of the need to protect the integrity of the investigation and the safety and privacy of third parties. Based on the Department’s experience in this matter, disclosure of investigative materials that identify or could reasonably lead to the identification of individuals associated with the investigation presents a heightened risk of undue harassment or interference, and would jeopardize ongoing investigative efforts.
For these reasons, and as outlined in the attached response, the Department maintains that the requested records are properly withheld under the applicable exemptions.
Thank you for your consideration. Please feel free to contact me should you require any additional information.
Respectfully,
Ken Fitzgerald, MPA
Chief of Police
Bellingham Police Department
30 Blackstone Street • Bellingham, MA 02019

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Can you please provide the number of total buses serving students with special needs,  number of total buses serving students with special needs equipped with Verra Mobility stop arm cameras,  number of total school buses and number total buses equipped with Verra Mobility (also known as American Traffic Solutions) stop arm cameras?

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

communications.subject: RE: [PRR 2526-102] Washington Public Records Act Request: Verra Special Needs Busing (Seattle Public Schools)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Below is the info from staff:


  *   We have contracted with two companies to provide bus service for students with special needs, and at this time the number of buses we utilize for that purpose is 217.  None of these are equipped with stop arm cameras as we are still working out the details of any such arrangement with the King County Sheriff’s Office.
  *   That said, we have made progress on this project and will be installing the cameras soon and that they will be installed on all the buses we use with First Student and Zum
  *   Our contractors currently utilize 127 Gen Ed buses and Verra is active installing cameras as we speak, to be completed within the next month.


The District does not intend to further address this request and the applicable statute of limitations for judicial review of the District’s decision will now begin to run.  If you feel we have misinterpreted the request or have any questions, or seek additional records, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

Thank you,


[SPS brandmark]

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Nevada County Sheriff)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Nevada County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: BRFSS Illinois

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: BRFSS Illinois
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Copies of the questionnaires distributed by Illinois DPH as part of the process of collecting data for the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System in 2024, including the questionnaires for optional modules. I am particularly interested in the questionnaire for the optional module on Sexual Orientation and Gender Identity (SOGI), but would like copies of the questionnaires for all optional modules administered by Illinois DPH that year.
2. Aggregated statistics of the data submitted by Illinois DPH to the CDC as part of the BRFSS data collection process from the SOGI module and any questions from the broader BRFSS survey that were broken down by gender (M/F/other). I do not need individual response level data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lena Bohman

Aggregated statistics of the data submitted by Illinois DPH to the CDC as part of the BRFSS data collection process from the SOGI module and any questions from the broader BRFSS survey that were broken down by gender (M/F/other). I do not need individual response level data.

You can download the information you are seeking from the following website: https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp. Questionnaires going back to 1998 are on the left dropdown. Summary data and demographic crosstabs are available by selecting a year and a topic.  The specific links to 2024<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/ques/2024IllinoisBRFSSquestionnaire.pdf> Questionnaire SOGI: Summary<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp?selTopic=_SEXOR&area=il&yr=2024&form=strata&show=freq>, Male Sexual Orientation<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp?xtabFile=_SEXOR_SOMALE&area=il&yr=2024&selTopic=_SEXOR&form=strata&show=xtab>, Female Sexual Orientation<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp?xtabFile=_SEXOR_SOFEMALE&area=il&yr=2024&selTopic=_SEXOR&form=strata&show=xtab>, Gender Identity<https://www.idph.state.il.us/brfss/statedata.asp?xtabFile=_SEXOR_TRNSGNDR2&area=il&yr=2024&selTopic=_SEXOR&form=strata&show=xtab>.

Sincerely,
Patricia C. Montalvo
Patricia C. Montalvo
Assistant General Counsel & FOIA Officer
Division of Legal Services
Illinois Department of Public Health
115 South LaSalle Street, 7th Floor
Chicago, IL 60603

Unauthorized use, disclosure or copying of this communication or any part thereof is strictly prohibited and may be unlawful. If you have received this communication in error, please notify the sender immediately by return e-mail and destroy this communication and all copies thereof, including all attachments. Receipt by an unintended recipient does not waive attorney-client privilege, attorney work product privilege, or any other exemption from disclosure.

title: Financial Report Request (University of Virginia)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Financial Report Request (University of Virginia)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request :: V005404-021826
communications.communication:
Hello Tom: I am writing to acknowledge receipt by the University of Virginia of your request for records under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), Va. Code § 2.2-3700 et seq., for the following: 
 
  *  
 To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined. 

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams. 

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time. 

Best, 

Greg Chick 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick Under FOIA, UVA has five working days to respond to your request for a response deadline of 2/25/2026.  If not practically possible to provide the requested records or to determine their availability within five working days, FOIA allows for UVA to request an additional seven working days.  Va. Code § 2.2-3704(A)(4).  If additional time is needed to fulfill your request, we will make reasonable efforts to reach an agreement with you for records production as required under FOIA.  Va. Code §§ 2.2-3700 and 2.2-3704(C). Please note that UVA may recover its actual costs incurred in processing your request.  This includes costs incurred in accessing, duplicating, supplying, or searching for the records you have requested, including, but not limited to, the costs of reviewing records to determine whether or not they are public records responsive to your request, exempt under FOIA, or may not lawfully be disclosed pursuant to some other provision of law (See Va. Code §2.2-3704(F)). If costs are estimated to exceed $200, we require advanced payment prior to fulfilling a request.  Va. Code § 2.2-3704(H).  If costs are estimated to be less than $200, actual costs incurred by UVA will be billed to you after you receive your response.  We will let you know of any costs in advance. For additional information about the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, please visit our website at https://universitycounsel.virginia.edu/foia/request-records. --- Please respond above this line --- 
Hello Tom: I am writing in response to your request for records under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), Va. Code § 2.2-3700 et seq., for the following: 
 
  *  
 To Whom It May Concern: 
 
Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 
 
1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined. 
 
2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams. 
 
Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time. 
 
Best, 
 
Greg Chick 
 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
 
Sincerely, 
 
Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick Attached you will find records responsive to your request.  We are awaiting receipt of ice hockey contracts. You may login to the University of Virginia Public Records Center to view and download the records: https://universitycounsel.virginia.edu/foia/request-records.  Your username is 204672-80255933@requests.muckrock.com.   If you are a first-time user, you will need to setup your password. 
This completes our response to your request.  Thank you for contacting this office. Kind Regards, FOIA Department Staff Office of University Counsel University of Virginia (434) 924-3586 foia@virginia.edu https://universitycounsel.virginia.edu/foia

title: Prop 36

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Prop 36
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking records related to the office's Prop 36 charging data. I am unsure what records the office possesses that would provide me with the following information: 
- Number of those being held or released who have been sentenced under Prop 36, including low-risk releases and anyone held due to the lack of treatment beds/available services. 
- Ages and races of those sentenced

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

J.

title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Nebraska Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Nebraska Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Nebraska Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nebraska Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: RE: Nebraska Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Nebraska Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
Dear Audrey Nielsen,

This email is regarding your records request received by our office on Thursday, February 19, 2026. We interpreted your request to be made under the Nebraska Public Records Statutes, Neb. Rev. Stat. §§ 84-712 through 84-712.09. Specifically, you requested the following:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.
2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.
3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.
This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

In response to your second request, please see the attached record. Regarding the remainder of your request, we have no responsive records.

If you have any questions related to this response, please email Colleen Byelick at colleen.byelick@nebraska.gov<mailto:colleen.byelick@nebraska.gov>.

The Elections Division
Nebraska Secretary of State's Office
Phone: (402) 471-2555

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Osceola County Sheriff's Department)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Osceola County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: PUBLIC RECORD REQUEST :: P039601-012926
communications.communication:
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the Osceola County Sheriff’s Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Chapter 119 of the Florida Statutes, the Public Records Act. Your request was received in this office on 1/29/2026 and given the reference number P039601-012926 for tracking purposes. 
 
Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2201982/ 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the records you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability of and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Florida Public Records Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you will receive an email notification when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
 
Osceola County Sheriff’s Office

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 
Sincerely, 
Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Composite Sketch Released June 15, 2016

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Composite Sketch Released June 15, 2016
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any information on the previously unreleased composite sketch jointly released by the Sacramento County Sheriff's Department and the FBI at a press conference on June 15, 2016. Specifically, I would like to know which case the composite sketch came from. There were 3 composite sketches released to the public at the press conference. The first one came from the 2/2/1978 Maggiore homicide and was released to the public on April 16, 1978. The second one came from the Rod Miller shooting and had been released sometime after 2001.  The third one had been previously unreleased and I would like to know which case the composite sketch came from.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Neville

title: To Date, 2025-2026 School Assignment

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: To Date, 2025-2026 School Assignment
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

In the CPS Procedural Manual, it states, "The IEP team then submits a school assignment request to ODLSS to provide the appropriate school closest to the student’s residence that can implement the IEP."

Provide records sufficient to show the following:

1. Anonymized data of all school assignment requests made on behalf of CPS students with IEPs requiring placement for the 2025-2026 (to date) school year. These records should include a unique non-identifiable code for the student, the date their IEP was finalized, the date the school assignment request was made, the date the school assignment letter was issued, and the date the student enrolled.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N018913-012526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
02/24/2026 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N018913-012526 
Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: 
To Whom It May Concern: 
Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 
In the CPS Procedural Manual, it states, "The IEP team then submits a school assignment request to ODLSS to provide the appropriate school closest to the student’s residence that can implement the IEP." 
Provide records sufficient to show the following: 
1. Anonymized data of all school assignment requests made on behalf of CPS students with IEPs requiring placement for the 2025-2026 (to date) school year. These records should include a unique non-identifiable code for the student, the date their IEP was finalized, the date the school assignment request was made, the date the school assignment letter was issued, and the date the student enrolled. 
This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
Sincerely, 
Miriam Bhimani 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2199523/ 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please be advised that the District does not comprehensively track the date a student enrolled as a result of a school assignment request. Thus, this portion of your request is asking the District to create a new record to answer your question. The Freedom of Information Act requires a public body to provide existing public records, subject to certain exceptions. The request must reasonably describe the requested records. A reasonable description requires the record to be reasonably identified as a record not as a general request for data, information and statistics to be gleaned generally from documents which have not been created and which the [public body] does not generally create. (See Krohn v. Department of Justice, 628 F.2d 195 (1980)). Second,  FOIA obligations apply to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request. Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32,    540 N.E.2d 11, 13 (1989) (quoting Krohn v. Department of Justice, 628 F.2d 195, 198 (D.C. Cir. 1980)). This request requires CPS to not merely pull data out of a database and provide it to you, but instead asks CPS to compile data from multiple sources into a single record, manipulate it to answer your questions, and this combination of data does not already exist in any CPS database. (See generally Hites v. Waubonsee Comm. College, Il App 2d, 150836 (2016)(... "requests went beyond a search for records—that is, the data in the databases—and instead improperly sought information about those records." quoting National Security Counselors v. Central Intelligence Agency, 898 F. Supp. 2d 233, 271 (D.D.C. 2012) and Chicago Tribune v. DFPR, IL App. (4th) 130437 at 8 (2014) ("[t]he Department was not obligated ... to answer plaintiff's "general inquiry question" concerning the number of initial claims since this would have required creating a new record.") 
You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Marisol Muneton Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

Reference: Rutgers Open Public Records Request, # R010421-121725 
Dear Mrs. Yasinski, 
Please accept this letter as the University Custodian of Records response to your request for records pursuant to the Open Public Records Act (“OPRA”). On December 17, 2025, this office received your request for the following: 
“To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Communications, including emails, reports, texts, phone records, between New Jersey Poison Information and Education System (NJPIES) and the FDA or CDC referencing yellow oleander, tejocote, tejocote root, hawthorn root, raiz de tejocote, or brazil nut supplements from Sept 7, 2022 to Dec 1, 2025. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Emma Yasinski 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2158901/” 
The University has reviewed your updated request. The responsive document(s) is now available to you through the Rutgers University Open Records Center. Please log in to the Portions of the documents you seek are advisory, consultative and deliberative. OPRA specifically states that government records “shall not include inter-agency or intra-agency advisory, consultative, or deliberative material.” Records Center and "My Records Center" to retrieve the appropriate responsive document(s). The document(s) is also attached to this email for your convenience.  N.J.S.A. 47:1A-1.1. Courts have construed this exemption to encompass the deliberative process privilege. See Ciesla v. N.J. Dept. of Health & Senior Servs., 429 N.J. Super. 127, 137 (App. Div. 2012). That privilege “‘permits the government to withhold documents that reflect advisory opinions, recommendations, and deliberations comprising part of a process by which governmental decisions and policies are formulated.’” Education Law Center. v. New Jersey Dept. of Education, 198 N.J. 274, 285-86 (2009) (quoting In re Liquidation of Integrity Ins. Co., 165 N.J. 75, 83 (2000)). Moreover, the deliberative process privilege is absolute, as OPRA “contains no limitation or qualification on this exemption.” Ciesla, 429 N.J. Super. at 143. 

Moreover, the Courts have consistently held that the advisory, consultative and deliberative exemption in OPRA is equivalent to the deliberative process privilege that exempts pre-decisional documents from public disclosure. OPRA thus “shields from disclosure documents ‘deliberative in nature, containing opinions, recommendations, or advice about agency policies,’ and ‘generated before the adoption of an agency’s policy or decision.’” Bent v. Stafford Police Department, 381 N.J. Super. 30, 37 (App. Div. 2005)(quoting Gannet New Jersey Partners LP v. County of Middlesex, 379 N.J. Super. 205, 219 (App. Div. 2005)). 

If your request has been denied, you may take your appeal to the New Jersey Government Records Council (NJGRC), which has mediation and dispute resolution procedures, or you may file a lawsuit with the New Jersey Superior Court, as provided by N.J.S.A. 47:1A-1, et seq. Your rights of appeal is provided with this letter at Public Facing Pages of the Rutgers University Open Public Records Request Center . You may also review the brochure at the Rutgers Custodian of Records page or visit the NJGRC page. 
If you have any questions, or wish to discuss this further, you may contact my office. 

Sincerely, 
Mary Ann Keys 
Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey

title: Marvin Day interaction reports

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Marvin Day interaction reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Regarding an interaction with Marvin Day on 07/02/2015:
- All incident, dispatch log entries and arrest reports. Incident reports and case records refer to reports produced with notes and narratives, a description of what happened by the responding officer, trace reports, case notes and litigation files
- All unedited body-worn camera / bodycam recordings. I am specifically requesting the unedited body-worn camera recordings captured by all officers present for the full duration of the incident, including audio recordings associated with the footage. If any portion of the requested footage must be redacted, I ask that you clearly indicate the portions that have been redacted and provide an explanation of the legal basis for the redactions
- All 911 or calls for service logs, audio and transcripts
- All computer aided dispatch (CAD) reports and a copy of your department’s dispatch 10 signals/codes, agency dispatch signals/codes and CAD signals/codes

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, I additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

I am a member of the news media. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Production of Responsive Records on a Rolling Basis: In the event that a portion of the present request is completed before the full request is completed, I ask that the records be produced on a rolling basis.

Request for Explanation of Withholdings and Redactions: If this request is denied in whole or in part, please provide a reasonable description of any withheld materials and a justification for all such withholdings that includes reference to the specific  exemptions authorizing withholding and specific reasons why such exemptions apply. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin
