communications.subject: San Francisco Police Public Records Request :: P218832-081025
communications.communication:
Dear Dillon Bergin: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the San Francisco Police Department ("SFPD"). 
 
The San Francisco Police Department ("SFPD") received your request, dated August 10, 2025 and given the reference number P218832-081025 for tracking purposes. 
 
Record(s) Requested: To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Regarding an interaction with Marvin Day on 07/02/2015:
- All incident, dispatch log entries and arrest reports. Incident reports and case records refer to reports produced with notes and narratives, a description of what happened by the responding officer, trace reports, case notes and litigation files
- All unedited body-worn camera / bodycam recordings. I am specifically requesting the unedited body-worn camera recordings captured by all officers present for the full duration of the incident, including audio recordings associated with the footage. If any portion of the requested footage must be redacted, I ask that you clearly indicate the portions that have been redacted and provide an explanation of the legal basis for the redactions
- All 911 or calls for service logs, audio and transcripts
- All computer aided dispatch (CAD) reports and a copy of your department’s dispatch 10 signals/codes, agency dispatch signals/codes and CAD signals/codes

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, I additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

I am a member of the news media. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Production of Responsive Records on a Rolling Basis: In the event that a portion of the present request is completed before the full request is completed, I ask that the records be produced on a rolling basis.

Request for Explanation of Withholdings and Redactions: If this request is denied in whole or in part, please provide a reasonable description of any withheld materials and a justification for all such withholdings that includes reference to the specific exemptions authorizing withholding and specific reasons why such exemptions apply.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fsan-francisco-police-department-367%252Fmarvin-day-interaction-reports-190925%252F%253Femail%253Dsanfranciscopd%252540govqa.us&url_auth_token=AAAIhlqz_6HzFpF9QbtVNXCgHbA%3A1ulIvR%3Adk9kil_L-v9wyio-oZwz0jAJU_9Tm1wSUcZj-3U-7Eg 
 
Your request is being forwarded to the appropriate department(s) for processing and you will be notified once the request is complete. 
 
You can monitor request progress at the link below.  Thank you for using the San Francisco Public Records Center. 
 
 
San Francisco Police Department

communications.communication:
November 11, 2025 
190925-42245323@requests.muckrock.com 
Dillon Bergin 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR190925 263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA 02115 
RE: Public Records Request, dated August 10, 2025, Reference # P218832-081025 
Dear Dillon Bergin: 
The San Francisco Police Department (SFPD) received your Public Records Act request, dated August 10, 2025. 
You requested, "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Regarding an interaction with Marvin Day on 07/02/2015: 
- All incident, dispatch log entries and arrest reports. Incident reports and case records refer to reports produced with notes and narratives, a description of what happened by the responding officer, trace reports, case notes and litigation files 
- All unedited body-worn camera / bodycam recordings. I am specifically requesting the unedited body-worn camera recordings captured by all officers present for the full duration of the incident, including audio recordings associated with the footage. If any portion of the requested footage must be redacted, I ask that you clearly indicate the portions that have been redacted and provide an explanation of the legal basis for the redactions 
- All 911 or calls for service logs, audio and transcripts 
- All computer aided dispatch (CAD) reports and a copy of your department’s dispatch 10 signals/codes, agency dispatch signals/codes and CAD signals/codes 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, I additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations. 

I am a member of the news media. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

Production of Responsive Records on a Rolling Basis: In the event that a portion of the present request is completed before the full request is completed, I ask that the records be produced on a rolling basis. 

Request for Explanation of Withholdings and Redactions: If this request is denied in whole or in part, please provide a reasonable description of any withheld materials and a justification for all such withholdings that includes reference to the specific exemptions authorizing withholding and specific reasons why such exemptions apply. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Dillon Bergin

files.title: 218832_Response
files.source: San Francisco Police Department, San Francisco, CA

title: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robin Monks

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Lynnwood, WA public records request #25-1237
communications.communication:
City of Lynnwood, WA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-1237: RE: Notice of Installment - Public Records Request Dear Requestor, The City of Lynnwood is writing in response to your above-referenced records request. A portion of the records responsive to your request is now available. An installment of records identified as responsive to your request is accessible to view and download at the City’s Records Portal. If you chose to pickup documents, they will be available at the Lynnwood Police Department during business hours. If you chose mailing of documents, they have been mailed with this letter. The City of Lynnwood requires additional time to: 1. Identify responsive records; 2. Assemble responsive records; and/or 3. Review the responsive records to determine whether any of the information is exempt from production. At this time, we believe that either another installment, or the remaining records responsive to your request, will be available on or before Nov 27, 2025. We will provide you with a status report on or before that date. Please note that the responsive records may contain information which is exempt from public disclosure. Information that is exempt from public disclosure is redacted. The reason for redaction has been identified with specificity in an exemption log which has been included with the records responsive to your request. Should you have any questions, please feel free to respond to this email through your portal account, or contact Police Records at (425) 670-5600. Respectfully, Lynnwood Police Records ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Lynnwood, WA.

Technical support: See our help page

Following a reasonable search, the Police Department identified records that are responsive to your request. The responsive records are available for you to review and download at the City's Records Portal. If you chose to pickup documents, they will be available at the Lynnwood Police Department during business hours. If you chose mailing of documents, they have been mailed with this letter. There is a one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review under the Public Records Act, which has started to run because the Department does not intend to further address this request. Please note that staff do not routinely monitor closed requests, however, you may ask follow-up questions regarding this request. If you have questions or believe we have misinterpreted your request, please feel free to contact the Lynnwood Police Department at (425)670-5600. title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Albany County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Albany County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Financial Report Request (Old Dominion University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Financial Report Request (Old Dominion University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Request for Math department records (California Polytechnic University Pomona)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Request for Math department records (California Polytechnic University Pomona)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All documents and records from the past two (2) years related to any agreements between the University and any of the following vendors in connection with usage for Math courses at the University:
* Cengage (aka Cengage Learning, Inc.), including any contracts related to WebAssign.
* Pearson (aka Pearson Education, Inc.), including any contracts related to MyLab.
* Wiley (aka John Wiley & Sons, Inc.) including any contracts related to WileyPlus or Knewton/Knewton Alta.
* McGraw Hill (aka McGraw Hill LLC, formerly McGraw-Hill Education, Inc.), including any contracts related to ALEKS.
* Lumen (aka Lumen Learning, Inc.), including any contracts related to Lumen OHM

2. All records identifying any individual who served as a course coordinator for any Math course at the University during the past two (2) years. This request is limited to official role-based information, including names, titles, and institutional (work) contact information of individuals assigned as course coordinators or holding equivalent coordinator responsibilities within the Math department. This request does not seek personal information beyond what appears in institutional records relating to job duties or course assignments. 

If any responsive records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide the specific legal basis for each withholding and a description of the withheld material as required under applicable public records law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sidhanth Grover

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Request for Math department records (California Polytechnic University Pomona)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following California Public Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Nov. 24, 2025. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Public Records Act Request: Grover, Sidhanth_PRA_20251124
communications.communication:
[cid:image001.png@01DC6512.25BFB650]
|    3801 W. Temple Ave, Pomona, CA 91768


December 4, 2025

RE: Public Records Act Request

Dear Sidhanth Grover,

We received your November 24, 2025, Public Records Act request for the following records:

1. All documents and records from the past two (2) years related to any agreements between the University and any of the following vendors in connection with usage for Math courses at the University:
* Cengage (aka Cengage Learning, Inc.), including any contracts related to WebAssign.
* Pearson (aka Pearson Education, Inc.), including any contracts related to MyLab.
* Wiley (aka John Wiley & Sons, Inc.) including any contracts related to WileyPlus or Knewton/Knewton Alta.
* McGraw Hill (aka McGraw Hill LLC, formerly McGraw-Hill Education, Inc.), including any contracts related to ALEKS.
* Lumen (aka Lumen Learning, Inc.), including any contracts related to Lumen OHM

2. All records identifying any individual who served as a course coordinator for any Math course at the University during the past two (2) years. This request is limited to official role-based information, including names, titles, and institutional (work) contact information of individuals assigned as course coordinators or holding equivalent coordinator responsibilities within the Math department. This request does not seek personal information beyond what appears in institutional records relating to job duties or course assignments.


Cal Poly Pomona has conducted a reasonable search for these records under California Government Code §7922.535 and has determined that your request, either in whole or in part, seeks copies of disclosable public records. We will make available to you any that are public Cal. Govt. Code §§7927.700 and 7922.000.  Records subject to the following exemptions will be withheld or redacted as appropriate:

  *   Vendor Identification Number pursuant to Cal. Govt. Code §7928.715
  *   General Privacy Rights. Cal. Govt. Code §7927.705; Cal. Const. Art. 1, §1.
  *   Balancing Test. Records where the public interest against disclosure outweighs the public interest in disclosure. Cal. Govt. Code §7922.000.

We will let you know when we have gathered all the records. It will be necessary to redact from them any personal and otherwise privileged information before they can be made available to you. Cal Poly Pomona estimates the records will be available January 29, 2025.

If you wish to inspect the records at Cal Poly Pomona you may contact publicrecords@cpp.edu<publicrecords@cpp.edu%20> to schedule a mutually convenient date and time. If you wish to receive copies instead, Cal Poly Pomona may charge the direct cost of duplication (20 cents per page). Once the records are located, we will advise you as to the exact number of pages upon which any charge will be based. We will require full payment before we begin the redaction and duplication process.

Thank you,
CPP Public Records Request

Email: publicrecords@cpp.edu<mailto:publicrecords@cpp.edu>

This communication is intended only for the use of the individual or entity to which it is addressed. This message contains information from California State Polytechnic University, Pomona (Cal Poly Pomona), which may be privileged, confidential and/or exempt from disclosure under applicable law. If the reader of this communication is not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any dissemination or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. Although Cal Poly Pomona has taken reasonable precautions to ensure no viruses are present in this email, Cal Poly Pomona cannot accept responsibility for any loss or damage arising from the use of this email or attachments.

title: Mark Anthony Conditt and Austin Bombing (Texas Department of Public Safety)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Mark Anthony Conditt and Austin Bombing (Texas Department of Public Safety)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In 2018 a series of bombings were perpetuated by Mark Anthony Conditt. The investigation involved the FBI, Austin Police Department, and the Texas Department of Public Safety. I am requesting 

- Audio or direct transcription of the 25 minute video recording my Mark Anthony Conditt as referenced by Governor Greg Abbot and former Austin Police chief Brian Manley -
 https://web.archive.org/web/20180323120234/https://www.statesman.com/news/breaking-austin-bombing-suspect-dies-police-close-official-says/KZmUAGvKlNazDr31EzeUzI/

- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt. 

I ask that the fee be waived as this information is within the public interest and many documents were widely commented on despite them not being available to the public (namely the 25 minute recording) release and access of this information is crucial to fair and accurate reporting on this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Nate Zaffiro

communications.subject: RE: Texas Public Information Act Request #P004167-021626
communications.communication:
I will certainly follow up with APD, however, I kindly ask for some clarification on your statement. The collaboration of DPS in this investigation is well documented and of public knowledge. Specific evidence of DPS involvement is publicly available and has been released by the FBI available both at the following link and in the attached file.

(https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20%28Final%29/view) 

Page 6 of 68 reads "FBI ERT combined with ATF explosive examiners, Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS), and APD homicide detectives to process the interior and exterior of the residence.

Page 7 of 68 reads “FBI, APD SWAT, and DPS located Conditt in Round Rock, TX.”

Page 47 of 68 reads "With the Austin Police Department (APD) as the lead law enforcement agency o n the case, the FBI worked together with the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF), the U.S. Postal Service and the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) to support the operation from a joint command center at the Combined Transportation Emergency Communications Center (CTECC), located in Austin, Texas.”

In the course of this collaberation, can you confirm that the Texas Department of Public Safety created, recieved, or stored no files relevant or responsive to the following issues:

- Audio or direct transcription of the 25 minute video recording by Mark Anthony Conditt as referenced by Governor Greg Abbot and former Austin Police chief Brian Manley -

- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt.


I am struggling to understand how DPS could have played a role in “locat[ing] Conditt” as stated by the FBI and yet have no files relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Conditt. Clarification or responsive files is greatly appreciated.

- Nate Zaffiro

https://web.arch DPS only maintains the of the plane video that has already been made available to the public.  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MffPH4Q4hXc  This is accessible on the DPS You Tube Channel.  DPS does not maintain any other responsive records other than this video recording by DPS aircraft.  Recordings made by Conditt and any other records would be held by the Austin Police Department or the Federal Bureau of Investigation. For questions or additional information, please reply to this email. Sincerely, ML Calcote Sr. Assistant General Counsel

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Revenue Dept. retention schedule
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The most recent records retention schedule for the agency, which includes a list of documents kept by the agency and for how long each record is retained. This may be called a general records schedule, a records index, or something else. Please release this in a PDF (.pdf) if possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Curt Merrill

communications.subject: Formal Acknowledgement | Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Revenue Dept. retention schedule
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the Georgia Department of Revenue. Please allow this to serve as the Department's initial response to and formal acknowledgement of your open records request.

The Department will attempt to provide all requested records, but due to confidentiality law, it is possible that some items may not be disclosed. Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., the Department will provide our formal response within 7-10 business days.

Under Georgia law, an agency may impose a reasonable charge for the search, redaction, and production or copying cost of records responsive to an Open Records Request. The time and cost will be calculated by using the hourly salary of the lowest paid employee qualified to conduct the research of an Open Records Request, with no charge for the first fifteen minutes of employee time expended. DOR will provide an estimate to the requestor if the reasonably anticipated costs for Production Tasks exceed $25 and will defer the Production Tasks until the requestor agrees to pay estimated costs.

Public Information Office
Georgia Department of Revenue
2595 Century Parkway, NE | Atlanta, GA 30345
Email: ORR@dor.ga.gov<mailto:ORR@dor.ga.gov>
Phone: 678-536-9348
dor.georgia.gov

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Kitsap County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Kitsap County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: USMS Arrest Procedures Policy Directive{s}
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The USMS Policy Directive(s) concerning arrest procedures and what is supposed to  happen after the USMS makes an arrest without a warrant.  I have a copy of a document dated 7/24/08 entitled   "USMS  Directives " "8.3 arrests",  however, it is not published on the DOJ websites  nor are any documents about arrest procedures that date from before or after July 24, 2008.  Please send all versions of this document.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Kay Sieverding

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Collier County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Collier County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Deaths-in-custody

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Deaths-in-custody
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The database or records of all Texas law enforcement agencies deaths-in-custody up to the date this request is ultimately fulfilled, including all deaths-in-custody reported pursuant to Article 49.18 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, Section 39.05 of the Penal Code and Section 501.055 of the Government Code. Pursuant to Article 49.18(b) of the Code of Criminal Procedure. 

This data should be collected pursuant to the Deaths in Custody Reporting Act. Texas’ practices are outlined in detail in this report: https://bja.ojp.gov/doc/dcra-plan-tx.pdf I am requesting the data described in the “Reporting Methods” section of the report. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.communication: At this link, the data is described as a spreadsheet that both the Texas Justice Initiative and Bureau of Justice Assistance request https://texasjusticeinitiative.org/post/does-texas-accurately-report-its-custodial-deaths-to-the-feds. This is the data that I’m requesting.

communications.subject: [Records Center] Public Information Request :: R031083-021126
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Actually your request was very unclear. 
Please note, however, that the law making the custodial death reports public ONLY applies to the actual custodial death report.  That is the reason that the actual custodial death reports are published online.  Once the data is manipulated, the statute longer applies.  Accordingly, this office will have to review and redact information that is confidential by law.  If, like the Texas Justice Initiative, you accept a spreadsheet WITHOUT the summary field, the spreadsheet can be provided for free.  However, if you seek the spreadsheet WITH the summary field, there will be a SIGNFICANT charge.  Please advise.  Thank you. no 
Sincerely, 

June B. Harden 
Assistant Attorney General 
Assistant Public Information Coordinator 
Office of the Attorney General

communications.subject: RE: Texas Public Information Act Request #R031083-021126
communications.communication:
Hi,

I accept the spreadsheet without the summary field. 

Thanks,
Dillon

title: Merced County Probation: Graffiti Abatement Service Request Form Photographs

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Merced County Probation: Graffiti Abatement Service Request Form Photographs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs uploaded via the Merced County Probation Department's Graffiti Abatement Service Request Form website:

https://www.countyofmerced.com/forms.aspx?fid=125

This is just a request for the images uploaded, not for the identifying information in the form itself. 

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the Merced County Probation department. 

Other municipalities in California publicly display reported graffiti violations online. Thank you for the Merced County Probation Office's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Use of force data (2017-2024) (Tempe Police Department)

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Use of force data (2017-2024) (Tempe Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2024. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Surprise AZ Planning & Zoning, ICE Warehouse

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Surprise AZ Planning & Zoning, ICE Warehouse
communications.communication:
To the Public Records/FOIA Officer:
Under the Arizona Public Records Act, I am requesting an opportunity to obtain copies of public records relating to the property located at 13290 W Sweetwater Ave, Surprise, AZ 85379.
Specifically, I am requesting the following records created, submitted, or received between January 1, 2025 and the date this request is processed:

- All building permits, renovation permits, variance requests, or "Change of Use" applications filed for the aforementioned property.
- All code compliance inspection reports, certificates of occupancy, and violation notices regarding the property.
- Any site plans, architectural drawings, or engineering evaluations submitted by the property owner, their LLC, or the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) regarding retrofitting the facility.

To expedite this request and reduce the administrative burden on your office, I am requesting these records in their native electronic format (e.g., PDF for documents, .PST or .EML for emails) delivered via email or secure download link.

If any part of this request is denied or redacted, please provide a written response citing the specific statutory exemption being applied.

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me beforehand if the estimated cost will exceed $25. However, I also request a waiver of all fees, as the disclosure of this information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of local government operations and infrastructure planning.

Thank you for your prompt assistance and public service.

Sincerely,
Matthew Ruehle

communications.subject: City Records Request :: R000999-021926
communications.communication:
Thank you for submitting a Public Records Request. Active public records are in various locations within the city.  Your request was received in this office on 2/19/2026 and given the reference number R000999-021926 for tracking purposes. Requests are processed in the order received. The city requests that a reasonable amount of time be expected for responding to any request. Records Requested: To the Public Records/FOIA Officer: 
Under the Arizona Public Records Act, I am requesting an opportunity to obtain copies of public records relating to the property located at 13290 W Sweetwater Ave, Surprise, AZ 85379. 
Specifically, I am requesting the following records created, submitted, or received between January 1, 2025 and the date this request is processed: 

- All building permits, renovation permits, variance requests, or "Change of Use" applications filed for the aforementioned property. 
- All code compliance inspection reports, certificates of occupancy, and violation notices regarding the property. 
- Any site plans, architectural drawings, or engineering evaluations submitted by the property owner, their LLC, or the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) regarding retrofitting the facility. 

To expedite this request and reduce the administrative burden on your office, I am requesting these records in their native electronic format (e.g., PDF for documents, .PST or .EML for emails) delivered via email or secure download link. 

If any part of this request is denied or redacted, please provide a written response citing the specific statutory exemption being applied. 

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me beforehand if the estimated cost will exceed $25. However, I also request a waiver of all fees, as the disclosure of this information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of local government operations and infrastructure planning. 

Thank you for your prompt assistance and public service. 

Sincerely, 
Matthew Ruehle

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Orange County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Orange County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: [Records Center] SHERIFF FOIL REQUEST :: P002450-012926
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: SHERIFF FOIL REQUEST of January 29, 2026., Reference # P002450-012926. 
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe, 
Orange County received a FOIL request from you on January 29, 2026. Your request mentioned: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2201994/" Orange County has reviewed your request and has determined that some of the records requested, or parts of the records requested, are exempt from disclosure under FOIL for the following reasons: NY Public Officers Law §87 (2)(b); §89 (2)(b) – Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy 
 
  * Removal of Street Address 
  * Simplification (undetailed) of Medical Information. 
 NY Public Officers Law §89 (3)(a) – Request does not reasonably describe the records sought (records cannot be identified or retrieved with reasonable effort given the agency’s filing system) 
 
  * Prior to October of 2023, the Orange County Sheriff’s Office did not have a computer program with the capacity to extract and compile the statistical information you seek. Paper records prior to October 2023, are not indexed or maintained in a manner that permits the Sheriff’s Office to identify and retrieve the requested documents with reasonable effort. Locating such records would be tantamount to searching for a needle in a haystack, a task that Freedom of Information Law does not require an agency to undertake. Your request for records from 2017 - October 2023 is denied, as it does not “reasonably describe” the records sought. See Committee on Open Government FOIL Advisory Opinion 17989 (February 3, 2010) (“the agency is not required to search the haystack, even though it is known that needles are there, somewhere.)” Responsive non-exempt records and/or redacted records have been made available. Please log in to the FOIL Records Center at the following link to retrieve the responsive documents. SHERIFF FOIL REQUEST - P002450-012926 
Sincerely, 
Deputy Schmitt #247 
Orange County Sheriff's Office We do not waive other grounds of exemption by limiting our response to the above and reserve the right to assert such if this matter is appealed.  Should you wish to appeal this determination, you must do so in writing submitted within thirty (30) days of the date of this letter to the attention of: Richard B. Golden, County Attorney, FOIL Appeals Officer, Orange County Department of Law, Government Center, 255-275 Main Street, Goshen, New York 10924.

title: Financial Report Request (Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Financial Report Request (Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

communications.subject: FOIA 33704   RE: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Horry County Police Department)
communications.communication:
Mr. Sinyangwe,

Following up with your FOIA request (attached) it is anticipated that the actual cost of providing the records requested will amount to approximately $39,175.00. Please forward to us the amount of $9,793.75 [25% deposit] and, when received, we will begin the process of compiling records. Please remit payment as set forth above by cash, certified funds, company check or money order (made payable to Horry County Government). If costs should end up being less than the amount deposited, you will be refunded the difference. Should costs end up being more, you will need to pay the difference at or prior to the time of delivery.

Should we not receive your deposit within twenty (20) calendar days from the date of this correspondence, we will consider your request closed. Online and over the phone payments are not available at this time.

Please remit payment to:

Horry County Government
Public Information
P.O. Box 1236
c/o Aaron Spelbring
Conway, SC 29528

Due to the volume of records requested, it will likely take a significant amount of time to fulfill this request, over and above the time set forth in the SC FOIA for production. If you intend to proceed with your request, please advise at the time of deposit if you do not consent to an extension of time of up to 24 months for production. If you should not so advise, we will assume that you consent to such extension.

Thank you,

Aaron P. Spelbring | FOIA Manager
Horry County Government
Public Information Office
211 Beaty St., Conway, South Carolina 29526
Tel: (843) 915-8424 | Fax: (843) 915-6390 | Spelbring.Aaron@horrycountysc.gov<mailto:Spelbring.Aaron@horrycountysc.gov>
www.horrycountysc.gov<http://www.horrycountysc.gov/>

**Note – Our physical office address has changed. Please update your records accordingly if applicable**

All e-mail correspondence to and from this address may be subject to public disclosure under the South Carolina Freedom of Information Act (FOIA). This correspondence is intended exclusively for the individual or entity to which it is addressed and may contain information that is proprietary, privileged, confidential or otherwise legally exempt from disclosure.

In accordance with South Carolina law, any personal information (including, but not limited to, a person’s name, date of birth, social security number, home address, home telephone number, driver’s identification number, medical or disability information, financial information, etc...) obtained through the South Carolina Freedom of Information Act cannot be used for commercial solicitation purposes directed to any person in this State. Anyone violating this provision is guilty of a misdemeanor, and upon conviction, may be fined up to $500, imprisoned up to one year, or both.

title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Iowa Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Iowa Open Records Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Iowa Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: CO County CVR Request (2022, 2024) (Logan County Clerk & Recorder)

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: CO County CVR Request (2022, 2024) (Logan County Clerk & Recorder)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast in Logan County that is generated by the voting system.

In case it is helpful for your reference, you can find an example of these records in REDACTED format at this link on the Arapahoe County webpage: https://www.arapahoevotes.gov/transparency
If you follow the "Redacted Cast Vote Record (CVR)" link on that webpage, it will download a csv file with Arapahoe County's redacted CVR records (this: https://gis.arapahoegov.com/dl_data/cr_voter_data/redacted_cvr/REDACTED_CVR_Export_20241126111830_330948.zip.)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

title: FOIA Request – October 27, 2024 Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request – October 27, 2024 Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

February 21, 2026

FOIA Officer Regional Center for Animal Care & Protection (RCACP)
Melinda Rector
mrector@roanokecountyva.gov

Re: FOIA Request – Custody, Care, and Handling of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente), October 27, 2024

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I request records related to the custody, handling, and care of a black-and-white tuxedo cat named Valiente, rescued on October 27, 2024 at Towers Shopping Center, Roanoke, Virginia. This request is limited to records held by RCACP.

Please provide all records, electronic or paper, in the following categories:

1. Personnel / Custody Information
Names and positions of RCACP staff who handled, evaluated, or had custody of Valiente after the rescue.
Records identifying any staff who received, transferred, or otherwise maintained custody of the cat.

2. Intake / Care / Transfer Records
Intake logs, stray reports, kennel or holding records, and any custody forms for Valiente on or about October 27–30, 2024.
Records reflecting transfer to RCACP from Police, Fire-EMS, or other entities.
Records of veterinary care, evaluation, or treatment performed while the cat was in RCACP custody.
Records reflecting release, adoption, euthanasia, or continued custody.

3. Communications
Emails, memoranda, or other written communications referencing Valiente.
Communications between RCACP and Police, Fire-EMS, veterinary providers, or other entities regarding the cat.

4. Metadata / Additional Notes
If records exist electronically, please provide metadata showing creation, modification, or access, to the extent easily available.
Please first confirm which records exist and provide an estimate of any fees before producing records, so that I may consider narrowing the request if necessary.
If portions of records are exempt from disclosure, please identify the statutory exemption and provide all reasonably segregable portions.

Please note that communications with me around the October 27, 2024 incident may have used a different phone number than the one listed above; records associated with that number are responsive.

Thank you for your assistance in fulfilling this request.
Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr. 

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se
1328 Peachtree Street NE
Atlanta, Georgia 30309-3209
(404) 389-5642
csdelozier@pm.me

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr..

Mr. Nash/Mr. DeLozier - 

Attached please find email correspondence between Mr. DeLozier and RCACP regarding the report of his missing cat.  Unfortunately, we did not intake Valiente into the shelter system.  There are no other documents available from the Regional Center for Animal Care and Protection (RCACP).  I have reviewed both our shelter software for that time period as well as physical files; and there are no documents other than the emails from and to Mr. DeLozier that I have found that reference a  black/white Tuxedo female cat found in the general area of Towers Mall in Roanoke, Virginia. We are the municipal shelter for the City of Roanoke, Counties of Botetourt and Roanoke, and the Town of Vinton.  We do not have any authority regarding Animal Control efforts from the Animal Control Divisions of those respective agencies.  We are simply where the animals come to be housed for legal stray holds.  If you want specific reports from the City of Roanoke Police Department Animal Control Division, you would need to contact that agency directly.  I hope this information is helpful.

Sincerely,
Melinda W. Rector
Director of Operations, RCACP

	•	Incident reports

Case details:

communications.subject: Fw: Florida Sunshine Law Request: BWC for Jessica Copeland
communications.communication:
Good morning,

Attached is the invoice amount for your public records request.

Once we receive payment then we will start working on your request. Please keep in mind, the bodycam cannot be sent electronically. If you need the records mailed, an additional cost will be required for postage. You do have the option to provide your own return label via email or by mail. Please let me know if you will be making payment for this request and if you will be picking up the records, needing a quote for shipping, or providing your own label.

Thank you,


Nicole Bennett

Putnam County Sheriff’s Office

Office of Professional Standards

Administrative Assistant

Phone: (386) 329-0828
